Name: Turtwig
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The shell on its back is made of soil. On a very healthy Turtwig, the shell should feel moist.

Name: Grotle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It knows where pure water wells up. It carries fellow Pokémon there on its back.

Name: Torterra
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Some Pokémon are born on a Torterra’s back and spend their entire life there.

Name: Combee
Effects: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a set of three. When they sleep, they gather up and form a giant hive of 100 Combee.

Name: Vespiquen
Effects: Search your deck for as many Combee as you like and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It houses its colony in cells in its body and releases various pheromones to make those grubs do its bidding.

Name: Cherubi
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. Starly try to peck it off.

Name: Cherrim
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has any [G] Energy attached to it.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: If it senses strong sunlight, it opens its folded petals to absorb the sun’s rays with its whole body.

Name: Sewaddle
Flavor-Text: Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes out of leaves, it is a popular mascot for fashion designers.

Name: Swadloon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Preferring dark, damp places, it spends the entire day eating fallen leaves that lie around it.

Name: Leavanny
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard all Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves by using the sticky silk secreted from its mouth.

Name: Maractus
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it moves rhythmically, it makes a sound similar to maracas, making the surprised Pokémon flee.

Name: Foongus
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that cards and shuffles it into his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: It lures Pokémon with its pattern that looks just like a Poké Ball then releases poison spores.

Name: Amoonguss
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that cards and shuffles it into his or her deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Condition. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: It moves the caps on both arms and does a dance to lure prey. It prefers damp places.

Name: Moltres-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 40 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chimchar
Flavor-Text: It is very agile. Before going to sleep, it extinguishes the flame on its tail to prevent fires.

Name: Monferno
Effects: Your opponent Switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It skillfully controls the intensity of the fire on its tail to keep its foes at an ideal distance.

Name: Infernape
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attack. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses unique fighting moves with fire on its hands and feet. It will take on any opponent.

Name: Victini-EX
Effects: Search your deck for 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 50 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pansear
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Very intelligent, it roasts berries before eating them. It likes to help people.

Name: Simisear
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Does 20 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it gets excited, embers rise from its head and tail and it gets hot. For some reason, it loves sweets.

Name: Litwick
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its flame is usually out, but it starts shining when it absorbs life force from people or Pokémon.

Name: Lampent
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has no Energy attached to it, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard the top 4 cards of your deck. This attack does 50 damage times the number of [R] Energy cards discarded.
Flavor-Text: It draws in air through its tail, transforms it into fire, and uses it like a tongue. It melts Durant and eats them.

Name: Squirtle
Flavor-Text: It shelters itself in its shell, then strikes back with spouts of water at every opportunity.

Name: Articuno-EX
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Swinub
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Rooting the tip of its snout into the ground, it searches for food. Sometimes, it even digs up a hot spring.

Name: Piloswine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip 5 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: With its excellent sense of smell, it’s even able to find mushrooms that are buried under frozen ground.

Name: Mamoswine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 90 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: When the temperature rose at the end of the ice age, most Mamoswine disappeared.

Name: Lotad
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It looks like an aquatic plant and serves as a ferry to Pokémon that can’t swim.

Name: Lombre
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has a mischievous spirit. If it spots an angler, it will tug on the fishing line to interfere.

Name: Ludicolo
Ability-Effect: At any times between turns, heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If it hears festive music, all its muscles fill with energy. It can’t help breaking out into a dance.

Name: Carvanha
Flavor-Text: They swarm any foe that invades their territory. Their sharp fangs can tear out boat hulls.

Name: Sharpedo
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its fangs rip through sheet iron. It swims at 75 mph and is known as “The Bully of the Sea.”

Name: Manaphy
Ability-Effect: When this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, attach a [W] Energy card from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.

Name: Vanillite
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Theoretically, this Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. Their breath is -58° F.

Name: Vanillish
Flavor-Text: They cool down the surrounding air and create ice particles, which they use to freeze their foes.

Name: Vanilluxe
Effects: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards.

Name: Frillish
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If its veil-like arms stun and wrap a foe, that foe will be dragged miles below the surface, never to return.

Name: Jellicent
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, the Attacking Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its body is mostly seawater. It’s said there’s a castle of ships Jellicent have sunk on the seafloor.

Name: Cubchoo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Their snot is a barometer of health. When healthy, their snot is sticky and the power of their ice moves increases.

Name: Beartic
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.
Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: They love the cold seas of the north. They create pathways around the ocean waters by freezing their own breath.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

Name: Magnemite
Flavor-Text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

Name: Magneton
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The stronger electromagnetic waves from the three linked Magnemite are enough to dry out surrounding moisture.

Name: Magneton
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The stronger electromagnetic waves from the three linked Magnemite are enough to dry out surrounding moisture.

Name: Magnezone
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may play 2 Supporter cards.
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Sometimes the magnetism emitted by Magnezone is too strong, making them attract each other so they cannot move.

Name: Magnezone
Effects: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Sometimes the magnetism emitted by Magnezone is too strong, making them attract each other so they cannot move.

Name: Zapdos-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 40 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rotom
Effects: Discard a [L] Energy card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.
Your opponent reveals his or her hand. This attack does 20 damage times the number of Trainer cards in your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Its electric-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

Name: Joltik
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Since it can’t generate its own electricity, it sticks onto large-bodied Pokémon and absorbs static electricity.

Name: Galvantula
Effects: Discard all [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It creates barriers from electrified silk that stun foes. This works as a weapon as well as a defense.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It does not need eyes, because it emits ultrasonic waves to check its surrounding while it flies.

Name: Zubat
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has no Energy attached to it, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It does not need eyes, because it emits ultrasonic waves to check its surrounding while it flies.

Name: Golbat
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Flitting around in the dead of night, it sinks its fangs into its prey and drains a nearly fatal amount of blood.

Name: Crobat
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Flavor-Text: Having four wings allows it to fly more quickly and quietly so it can sneak up on prey without its noticing.

Name: Koffing
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Toxic gas is held within its thin, balloon-shaped body, so it can cause massive explosions.

Name: Koffing
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Toxic gas is held within its thin, balloon-shaped body, so it can cause massive explosions.

Name: Weezing
Ability-Effect: When this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Inhaling toxic fumes from trash and mixing them inside its body lets it spread an even fouler stench.

Name: Ralts
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

Name: Gallade
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When trying to protect someone, it extends its elbows as if they were swords and fights savagely.

Name: Giratina
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 50 more damage.
Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.

Name: Trubbish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: The combination of garbage bags and industrial waste caused the chemical reaction that created this Pokémon.

Name: Trubbish
Flavor-Text: Wanting more garbage, they follow people who litter. They always belch poison gas.

Name: Trubbish
Effects: Does 20 damage for each Pokémon Tool card attached to Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: Wanting more garbage, they follow people who litter. They always belch poison gas.

Name: Garbodor
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.

Name: Garbodor
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of [C] in the Defending Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Consuming garbage makes new kinds of poison gases and liquids inside their bodies.

Name: Elgyem
Flavor-Text: Rumors of its origin are linked to a UFO crash site in the desert 50 years ago.

Name: Elgyem
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Rumors of its origin are linked to a UFO crash site in the desert 50 years ago.

Name: Beheeyem
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Move 6 damage counters from any of your Pokémon to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three fingers, but those patterns haven’t been decoded.

Name: Phanpy
Flavor-Text: It is strong despite its compact size. It can easily pick up and carry an adult human on its back.

Name: Donphan
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 60 more damage. Discard the Stadium card.
Flavor-Text: It attacks by curling up, then rolling into its foe. It can blow apart a house in one hit.

Name: Lunatone
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.
Flavor-Text: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It’s active on the night of a full moon.

Name: Solrock
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
This Pokémon does 90 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Solar energy is the source of its power, so it is strong during the daytime. When it spins, its body shines.

Name: Riolu
Flavor-Text: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions such as joy and rage in the form of waves.

Name: Riolu
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It uses the shapes of auras, which change according to emotion, to communicate with others.

Name: Lucario
Flavor-Text: A well-trained one can sense auras to identify and take in the feelings of creatures over half a mile away.

Name: Lucario
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [M] Energy attached to it, this Pokémon’s type is both [F] and [M].
Effects: Does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
Flavor-Text: By reading the auras of all things, it can tell how others are feeling from over half a mile away.

Name: Timburr
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Always carrying squared logs, they help out with construction. As they grow, they carry bigger logs.

Name: Gurdurr
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: With strengthened bodies, they skillfully wield steel beams to take down buildings.

Name: Conkeldurr
Effects: If this Pokémon is Burned or Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.
Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Rather than rely on force, they master moves that utilize the centrifugal force of spinning concrete.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its cute act is a ruse. When victims let down their guard, they find their items taken. It attacks with sharp claws.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Their cute act is a ruse. They trick people and steal their valuables just to see the looks on their faces.

Name: Liepard
Effects: Your opponent reveal his or her hand. Discard a Supporter card you find there. Use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Their beautiful form comes from the muscles they have developed. They run silently in the night.

Name: Scraggy
Flavor-Text: Proud of its sturdy skull, it suddenly headbutts everything, but its weight makes it unstable, too.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It pulls up its shed skin to protect itself while it kicks. The bigger the crest, the more respected it is.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: Discard a Team Plasma card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its heavy-looking iron body is actually thin and light, so it can fly at speeds over 180 mph.

Name: Klink
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Two bodies comprise a fixed pair. They spin around each other to generate energy.

Name: Klang
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: A minigear and big gear comprises its body. If the minigear it launches at its foe doesn’t return, it will die.

Name: Klinklang
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to your [M] Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon-EX.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The minigear spins at high speed. Then the energy from the red core charges the minigear to make it ready to fire.

Name: Durant
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Individuals each play different roles in driving Heatmor, their natural predator, away from their colony.

Name: Durant
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Individuals each play different roles in driving Heatmor, their natural predator, away from their colony.

Name: Cobalion-EX
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Druddigon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It runs through the narrow tunnels formed by Excadrill and Onix. It uses its sharp claws to catch prey.

Name: Black Kyurem-EX
Effects: Discard 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: White Kyurem-EX
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: On nights with a full moon, Clefairy gather from all over and dance. Bathing in moonlight makes them float.

Name: Clefable
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Their ears are sensitive enough to hear a pin drop from over a mile away, so they’re usually found in quiet places.

Name: Doduo
Effects: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with telepathic power.

Name: Dodrio
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: When Doduo evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.

Name: Snorlax
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent’s Active Pokémon can’t retreat.
Effects: Does 30 damage times the number of Team Plasma Pokémon you have in play.
Flavor-Text: When its belly is full, it becomes too lethargic to even lift a finger, so it is safe to bounce on its belly.

Name: Togepi
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It transforms the kindness and joy of others into happiness, which it stores in its shell.

Name: Togetic
Effects: Your opponent draws a card.
Flavor-Text: To share its happiness, it flies around the world seeking kind-hearted people.

Name: Togekiss
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent plays an Item card from his or her hand, prevent all effects of that card done to your Pokémon.
Effects: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: It shares many blessings with people who respect one another’s rights and avoid needless strife.

Name: Whismur
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Usually, its cries are like quiet murmurs. If frightened, it shrieks at the same volume as a jet plane.

Name: Loudred
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The shock waves from its cries can tip over trucks. It stamps its feet to power up.

Name: Exploud
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Discard all Item cards you find there.
Does 50 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.
Flavor-Text: Its howls can be heard over six miles away. It emits all sorts of noises from the ports on its body.

Name: Lugia-EX
Ability-Effect: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Effects: Discard a Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you can’t discard a Plasma Energy, this attack does nothing.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Skitty
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It shows its cute side by chasing its own tail until it gets dizzy.

Name: Patrat
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Extremely cautious, one of them will always be on the lookout, but it won’t notice a foe coming from behind.

Name: Patrat
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Extremely cautious, one of them will always be on the lookout, but it won’t notice a foe coming from behind.

Name: Watchog
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Using luminescent matter, it makes its eyes and body glow and stuns attacking opponents.

Name: Watchog
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using luminescent matter, it makes its eyes and body glow and stuns attacking opponents.

Name: Bouffalant
Effects: Discard a Pokémon Tool card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They are known to charge so wildly that if a train were to enter their territory, they would send it flying.

Name: Rufflet
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It stands up to massive opponents, not out of courage, but out of recklessness. But that is how it gets stronger.

Name: Braviary
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Effects: You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The more scars they have, the more respect these brave soldiers of the sky get from their peers.

Name: Bicycle
Description: Draw cards until you have 4 cards in your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Colress
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Colress Machine
Description: Search your deck for a Plasma Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Team Plasma Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Escape Rope
Description: Each player switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon. (Your opponent switches first. If a player does not have a Benched Pokémon, he or she doesn’t switch Pokémon.)
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Ether
Description: Reveal the top card of your deck. If that card is a basic Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If it is not a basic Energy card, return it to the top of your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Eviolite
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is a Basic Pokémon, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Hypnotoxic Laser
Description: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is also Asleep.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Plasma Frigate
Description: Each Pokémon that has any Plasma Energy attached to it (both yours and your opponent’s) has no Weakness.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Team Plasma Grunt
Description: Discard a Team Plasma card from your hand. (If you can’t discard a Team Plasma card, you can’t play this card.) Draw 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Virbank City Gym
Description: Put 2 more damage counters on Poisoned Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) between turns.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Dowsing Machine
Description: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can’t discard 2 cards, you can’t play this card.) Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Scramble Switch
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, you may move as many Energy attached to the old Active Pokémon to the new Active Pokémon as you like.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Victory Piece
Description: If this card is attached to Victini-EX, Victini-EX can use its attacks regardless of the amount or type of Energy attached to it.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Victini-EX
Effects: Search your deck for 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 50 more damage.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Articuno-EX
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cobalion-EX
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lugia-EX
Ability-Effect: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Effects: Discard a Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you can’t discard a Plasma Energy, this attack does nothing.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Colress
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Charizard
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Blastoise
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Random Receiver
Description: Reveal cards from the top of your deck until you reveal a Supporter card. Put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
